_meta.purpose: Code-grounded proportional + feature guide so a CSG agent authors RECOGNIZABLE, on-style models. Ship as plugin reference JSON.
_meta.units: All spatial values are RATIOS, not mm. Two reference units: H = head height (head-unit); T = total figurine height. Agent picks target T (or H), then multiplies. For animals/figures H is primary; for objects (rocket/tree/car) T is primary.

style_axis.realism: anatomically-weighted, smaller eyes, longer limbs, more primitives + hull/loft
style_axis.cartoonish: exaggerate the 2-3 signature cues, smooth blobs
style_axis.low_poly: replace smooth solids with prism/wedge/box facets, no fillets
style_axis.chibi: head 33-50% of T, 1:1 leg:head, thick stubby limbs, no neck
style_axis.geometric: pure primitives, minimal booleans, readable as logo silhouette

_meta.eye_rule: Cute/anime per-eye diameter = 0.18-0.28 of FACE WIDTH; both eyes + gap span 0.55-0.70 of face width. Realistic per-eye = 0.10-0.14 of face width. Eyes sit on the horizontal midline of the head (lower for cuter). Pupils carved as difference() spheres at 0.40-0.50 of eye diameter.
_meta.universal_method: 1) lock head-unit H and total T from style head-count. 2) block body mass. 3) place head. 4) add the 4-6 silhouette cues AS RATIOS OF H. 5) carve eyes/mouth via difference. 6) ensure a flat base (.on_bed) and >=2mm features for print. Silhouette test: does the BLACK OUTLINE alone read as the subject?

print_constraints.must_stand: widen base or add feet so center-of-mass is over footprint

_meta.owl_failure_diagnosis: The shipped owl uses absolute mm with no H-unit, eyes d=20 on head d=46 (0.43 of face, reads beady not cute), facial disc too thin, no silhouette checklist. Fix: head d -> 1.0H, eyes combined span 0.62*face, big facial disc, prominent ear tufts, beak 0.18H.

silhouette_cues: upright rounded egg body, no neck
large flat facial disc framing two big forward eyes
two ear tufts angled outward on top
short downward triangular beak between eyes

cartoon.eye_per_eye: 0.26 of face width; combined span 0.62 of face

owl.csg_recipe: body = ellipsoid(0.55H,0.48H,0.85H); head = sphere(d=1.0H) on top, union (no neck); facial_disc = flattened ellipsoid(0.50H wide,0.12H deep,0.42H tall) unioned on front of head; 2x eye = sphere(d=0.30H) raised on disc, then difference pupil sphere(d=0.13H); beak = cone(h=0.18H) rotate_x(-90) below eyes; 2x ear_tuft = cone(h=0.55H,d=0.18H) rotate_y(+/-18) on head top; 2x foot = ellipsoid at base; 2x wing = flattened ellipsoid(0.14H,0.36H,0.6H) hugging sides. .on_bed()
owl.pitfalls: Don't make eyes <0.5 combined face span (owl reads as generic bird). Tufts must clear head silhouette by >=0.3H.

cat.csg_recipe: body = capsule or two stacked spheres (bean); head = sphere(d=1.4H cartoon); 2x ear = cone(h=0.7H,d=0.4H base) OR wedge, on head top, rotate_y(+/-15); muzzle = small ellipsoid front; nose = tiny cone(h=0.06H) rotate_x(-90); tail = swept torus-arc or bent capsule(len 1.8H); 4x leg = capsule(d=0.3H). Whiskers: skip (too thin to print) or thick prism slivers >=2mm. .on_bed()
cat.pitfalls: Ears MUST be sharp triangles, not rounded (rounded ears -> reads as bear/dog). Keep ears separated, not fused into a single ridge.

silhouette_cues: ears: floppy down (hound) OR perked triangle (shepherd) - pick one
dog.csg_recipe: body = rounded_box or capsule(len 1.6H); head = sphere(d=1.5H); muzzle = ellipsoid(0.5H,0.4H,0.35H) unioned forward+down of head; nose = sphere(d=0.18H) on muzzle tip; 2x floppy_ear = flattened ellipsoid(0.15H,0.4H,0.7H) drooping at head sides OR 2x perked = cone; tail = bent capsule up; 4x leg = capsule(d=0.32H). .on_bed()
dog.pitfalls: Snout is THE dog cue - without a projecting muzzle a dog reads as a bear or generic blob. Don't give a cat-style slim body.

silhouette_cues: large pointed upright triangular ears (proportionally bigger than cat)
fox.csg_recipe: body = capsule(len 1.4H); head = sphere(d=1.4H) with wedge/ellipsoid snout forward; 2x ear = tall cone(h=0.95H,d=0.45H) on head top, more vertical than cat; tail = large bushy ellipsoid(0.7H,0.7H,1.6H) tapering, sweeping up behind; snout tip = small cone nose. .on_bed()
fox.pitfalls: The bushy oversized tail + big pointed ears separate fox from cat. Make tail diameter clearly fatter than cat (0.5-0.7H).

silhouette_cues: two TALL upright ears (dominant cue, longest feature)
small round cotton tail at back
short front paws, larger hind feet
rabbit.csg_recipe: body = sphere/ellipsoid(1.2H) crouched; head = sphere(d=1.3H) front-top, slight union; 2x ear = capsule or flattened ellipsoid(0.25H wide,0.12H deep,1.6H tall) standing vertical, slight V-splay; nose = tiny sphere; cotton tail = sphere(d=0.3H) at rear; 2x hind_foot = ellipsoid flat at base for standing; 2x front_paw = small capsule. .on_bed()
rabbit.pitfalls: Ears must be the tallest element and clearly longer than the head, or it reads as a cat/mouse. Keep ears vertical not floppy (floppy = dog).

silhouette_cues: small ROUND ears low/wide on a big round head
bear.csg_recipe: body = large sphere or rounded_box(barrel) 1.8H wide; head = sphere(d=1.5H); 2x ear = sphere(d=0.5H) ROUND, half-embedded at head top corners; muzzle = short ellipsoid(0.3H) + nose sphere(d=0.15H); 4x limb = thick capsule(d=0.6H,len 0.7H); optional belly = lighter ellipsoid panel. .on_bed()
bear.pitfalls: Ears MUST be round spheres (pointed = cat/fox). Keep limbs thick and short; thin limbs make it read as a dog/human. Round everything - bear = softness.

silhouette_cues: clawed feet, often bipedal t-rex-like stance
dragon.csg_recipe: Base on t-rex body. body = ellipsoid(1.5H); neck = bent tube/capsule curving up to head = sphere(d=1.4H); 2x horn = cone(h=0.6H) swept back on head; 2x wing = membrane via loft or hull of 3-4 points, or flattened wedge fan; spinal_ridge = row of small cone/wedge spikes unioned along back via loop; tail = tapering bent tube 2-3H; 2x hind_leg = thick capsule with clawed foot; 2x tiny arm. .on_bed()
dragon.pitfalls: Without wings + horns + spinal spikes, a dragon reads as a t-rex or lizard. Wings are the #1 cue - make them large and visible in silhouette.

silhouette_cues: large head with big jaws + small triangle teeth
two powerful hind legs with chunky clawed feet
heavy horizontal tail balancing the head
bulky oval body tilted forward (spine roughly horizontal)

realistic.stance: horizontal spine, head forward over feet

trex.csg_recipe: body = large ellipsoid(1.4H) tilted forward; head = ellipsoid(1.3H) with jaw = lower ellipsoid, teeth = row of small cones via loop (difference for mouth gap); 2x arm = tiny capsule(0.3-0.4H) high on chest; 2x hind_leg = thick bent capsule(2H) with foot = flattened ellipsoid + 3 claw cones; tail = long tapering tube(2.5H) horizontal back. Balance over feet for standing. .on_bed()
trex.pitfalls: Tiny arms + big head + balancing tail are the trio. Don't stand it fully upright/vertical (that's the outdated Godzilla pose); modern read is horizontal spine. Keep arms ABSURDLY small.

silhouette_cues: dorsal fin on top + pectoral side fins
gill line + round eye on side of head
puckered or open mouth at front
fish.csg_recipe: body = ellipsoid(2.2H,0.6H,0.9H) teardrop (taper toward tail); tail_fin = flattened wedge or two triangles (prism) forked; dorsal_fin = wedge/prism on top edge; 2x pectoral_fin = thin flattened ellipsoid on sides; eye = sphere(d) on each side with carved pupil; mouth = small torus or difference notch at front; gill = shallow difference groove. Stand on a small base or lay flat. .on_bed()
fish.pitfalls: Body must taper toward the tail (teardrop), not be symmetric. Fins are thin - thicken to >=2mm or use wedge prisms for printability.

silhouette_cues: round/oval body with no visible neck (songbird)
fan or pointed tail angled up/back
round eye on side of head
bird.csg_recipe: body = ellipsoid/sphere(1.2-1.5H); head = sphere(d) merged on top-front (no neck for songbird); beak = cone(h=0.25-0.3H) rotate_x(-90) forward; 2x wing = flattened ellipsoid hugging sides, pointed back; tail = flattened wedge/prism fan angled up; 2x leg = thin capsule OR sit on belly base; 2x eye sphere on sides. .on_bed()
bird.pitfalls: Beak forward + tail up are minimal cues. Distinguish from owl: bird beak smaller/forward, eyes on SIDES (not big forward facial disc), no ear tufts.

silhouette_cues: two large bulging eyes ON TOP of a wide flat head
wide grinning mouth line across the front
long folded bent hind legs (knees up beside body)
frog.csg_recipe: body = wide flattened ellipsoid(1.4H,1.0H,0.8H) squat; head = front portion of body (low, wide); 2x eye = sphere(d=0.4-0.5H) UNIONED on TOP of head (bulging up), carve pupil; mouth = wide shallow difference cut (slot/wedge) across front; 2x hind_leg = bent capsules folded beside body, foot = splayed flattened ellipsoid with webbing; 2x front_foot = small splayed. Wide flat base. .on_bed()
frog.pitfalls: Eyes ON TOP of head (not on front) is THE frog cue - placing eyes on the front makes it read as a generic blob. Keep body wide and squat, never tall.

silhouette_cues: boxy/cylindrical head distinct from torso (visible neck joint)
rectangular boxy torso with chest panel
segmented cylindrical/boxy limbs with joint balls
hard 90-degree edges, panel lines, bolts - mechanical not organic

cartoon.eye: single visor or 2 round lights 0.25 of face

robot.csg_recipe: head = box or rounded_box or cylinder; neck = short cylinder; torso = box(1.2-1.8H) with chest_panel = shallow difference rectangle; 2x arm = cylinder segments + sphere joints (shoulder/elbow); 2x leg = box/cylinder segments + sphere knees; antenna = thin cylinder + sphere tip; eye = cylinder visor or 2 spheres; add bolts = small cylinders, panel_lines = shallow difference grooves. Flat box feet. .on_bed()
robot.pitfalls: Robots need HARD edges and visible joints - smooth blobby robots read as toys. Use box/cylinder, AVOID ellipsoid/sphere for body. Add at least 3 mechanical details (panel/bolt/antenna).

silhouette_cues: head with clear face plane (brow, nose ridge, chin)
shoulder/upper-chest mass cut flat at the bottom (bust base)
balanced facial feature placement on thirds

realistic.face_thirds: hairline->brow, brow->nose-base, nose-base->chin each = 1/3 of face height
realistic.eye_line: at vertical midline of head; eyes 1 eye-width apart; face = 5 eyes wide

human_bust.csg_recipe: skull = sphere slightly ellipsoidal; face = front carved/added planes: brow ridge (ellipsoid), nose = wedge/prism ridge forward, chin = lower ellipsoid; eyes = carved spheres on midline (difference sockets + sphere eyeballs); hair = larger sphere/loft shell unioned over back of skull (difference face area); neck = cylinder; shoulders = wide ellipsoid/loft; cut flat bottom with box difference for bust base. .on_bed()
human_bust.pitfalls: Feature placement on the thirds + eyes-at-midline is critical; misplaced eyes (too high) is the #1 uncanny error. The bottom MUST be cut flat (it's a bust, not a full body).

silhouette_cues: three stacked spheres, large->medium->small bottom to top
coal dots for eyes + smile (carved or small spheres)

realistic.total_H: sum of 3 diameters ~stacked with slight overlap

snowman.csg_recipe: bottom = sphere(d=8u); middle = sphere(d=5u) stacked (center at bottom_top - small overlap); head = sphere(d=3u) on top; union all three; nose = cone(h=0.5*head_d) rotate_x(-90) forward on head; 2x eye = small sphere or difference dot; mouth = row of small difference dots in arc; 2x arm = thin cylinder sticks angled out from middle; optional hat = cylinder + thin disc brim. .on_bed()
snowman.pitfalls: Spheres must clearly decrease in size bottom-to-top (equal spheres read as a caterpillar/blob). Overlap spheres ~10-15% so the union is solid and printable, not pinched.

silhouette_cues: rounded dome top (head) flowing into a wider body with NO neck
optional stubby side arms reaching out
ghost.csg_recipe: body = capsule or revolve of a bell profile (dome top -> flared skirt); OR sphere(head) unioned with a downward-flaring cone/tube; bottom_hem = subtract 3-5 spheres in a row along the base to create scallop waves (difference); 2x eye = difference oval sockets or small carved spheres; mouth = difference sphere/torus O-shape; optional 2x arm = capsule stubs. Hollow optional (tube) but keep walls >=2mm. Flat-ish base from scallop low points. .on_bed()
ghost.pitfalls: The scalloped wavy bottom hem is THE ghost cue - a flat-bottomed dome reads as a tooth or a generic blob. Carve 3+ waves.

silhouette_cues: dome/umbrella cap distinctly wider than stem
cylindrical stem narrower than cap, slightly bulged at base
spots on cap (toadstool) - raised or carved
gill lines under the cap rim
mushroom.csg_recipe: cap = hemisphere (sphere clipped flat, or revolve of a dome arc); stem = cylinder(d=0.4*cap_d, h=stem_H) with slight base_bulb = small sphere at bottom; union; spots = small sphere bumps unioned on cap top (or difference dimples); gills = radial difference grooves under cap rim via loop; ensure cap overhangs stem clearly. Flat base from stem bottom. .on_bed()
mushroom.pitfalls: Cap MUST overhang the stem with a clear lip (cap same width as stem = reads as a lollipop/nail). Cap overhang is an overhang for printing - keep underside angle reasonable or it needs support.

silhouette_cues: foliage mass clearly distinct from trunk
conifer = stacked cones/triangle; deciduous = round/cloud canopy on a clear trunk
trunk narrower than canopy, widens at root base
tapered top (pointed for pine, rounded for oak)

cartoon.deciduous: 1-3 overlapping spheres on a stubby trunk, lollipop

tree.csg_recipe_conifer: trunk = cylinder(short); foliage = 2-4 stacked cones decreasing in diameter going up (each base overlaps the one below); union. Low-poly: replace cones with prism (n=6). .on_bed()
tree.csg_recipe_deciduous: trunk = cylinder(d, h=0.4T) with root_flare = small cone at base; canopy = 1-3 overlapping spheres(d=0.5-0.7T) unioned on top; optional branch = thin cylinders. Low-poly: canopy = hull of several prisms. .on_bed()
tree.pitfalls: Trunk must be visibly thinner than canopy and the canopy must sit clearly above it. A cone with no trunk reads as a generic cone, not a tree - always show some trunk.

silhouette_cues: 2-4 triangular fins flaring at the base
optional flared nozzle + exhaust flames at bottom
rocket.csg_recipe: body = cylinder(d=0.25-0.35T, h=0.65T); nose = cone(h=0.2-0.25T, d=body_d) on top, union; 3-4x fin = wedge or prism triangles flaring out at base via rotational loop (rotate around axis); window = difference cylinder/sphere porthole (or raised torus ring); optional nozzle = inverted cone flare at bottom; flames (if wanted) = cone/teardrop union below, but usually omit for print. Stand on fins + body base. .on_bed()
rocket.pitfalls: Fins at the base are essential for the rocket read AND for standing stability. Nose must be a sharp cone, not rounded (rounded = pill/capsule). 3 fins prints/stands better than 4 for a flat-render silhouette.

silhouette_cues: horizontal body box longer than tall
cabin/greenhouse (windows) set on top, narrower than body
car.csg_recipe: body = rounded_box(len=1.0, w=0.4, h=0.28); cabin = smaller rounded_box on top, set back, taper front (windshield) via wedge difference; windows = shallow difference panels; 4x wheel = cylinder(d) rotate_x(90) at corners, recessed slightly; 4x wheel_arch = difference cylinder cut into body over each wheel; headlights = 2 small spheres/cylinders front; hood = slope via wedge. Wheels touch bed. .on_bed()
car.pitfalls: Cabin must be narrower and set back (not centered) to read as a car vs a box on wheels. Wheels must be visible past the body line. Add wheel arches or it looks like a brick.

silhouette_cues: upright bipedal stance with flat base for standing
readable pose (arms/legs in a recognizable gesture)
balanced mass so it stands without tipping
generic_figurine.csg_recipe: head = sphere(d=1.0H); body/torso = capsule or rounded_box (1.0H chibi / 2.5H realistic); 2x arm = capsule(thick for chibi); 2x leg = capsule, ending in flat ellipsoid feet for standing; optional neck = short cylinder (omit for chibi); hair/hat = sphere/loft over skull; carve face eyes via difference. Ensure feet form a stable footprint; widen stance if top-heavy. .on_bed()
generic_figurine.pitfalls: Pick ONE head-count and apply it to every limb (mixing chibi head with realistic legs looks broken). Must stand: keep center-of-mass over the foot footprint - widen feet or stance for big-headed chibi.
